title: Seismo-Turbidites in Aysén Fjord (Southern Chile) Reveal a Complex Pattern of Rupture Modes Along the 1960 Megathrust Earthquake Segment
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Seismo-turbidites in Aysén Fjord (southern Chile) are analyzed using sub-bottom profiler, sediment cores, and multibeam bathymetry to identify over 25 seismo-turbidites in the last 9,000 years. The majority are related to megathrust earthquakes, providing the first crucial paleoseismic data demonstrating that the 1960 Mw 9.5 Valdivia event was not unique for its segment. A recurrence rate of 321 ± 116 years is estimated for the last two millennia.

location.description: Aysén Fjord (~45.5°S) southern Chile; seismo-turbidite paleoseismic record from sub-bottom profiler, bathymetry, and sediment cores

seismic_lines.name: Sub-bottom profiler and bathymetry — Aysén Fjord ~45.5°S

data.name: Sub-bottom Profiler Data — Aysén Fjord ~45.5°S (seismo-turbidites)
data.description: TOPAS PS18 sub-bottom profiler data from Aysén Fjord (MD159-PACHIDERME cruise, 2007) imaging seismo-turbidite stratigraphy and mass transport deposit distribution; linked to 2007 Aysén earthquake and historical megathrust events. See Wils et al. (2018) for primary data publication.

data.name: Multibeam Bathymetry — Aysén Fjord ~45.5°S
data.description: ELAC SeaBeam 1050 multibeam bathymetry of Aysén Fjord mapping mass transport deposits and submarine landslide morphology related to the 2007 earthquake sequence. See Wils et al. (2018) for primary data publication.

data.name: Grain Size and Geochemical Data — MD07-3117 Sediment Core, Aysén Fjord
data.description: Grain size, magnetic susceptibility, CT scan, and radiocarbon data from core MD07-3117 (21 m long). Grain size end-member modeling, age model (Bacon), and turbidite identification results. Data Availability Statement confirms all data in manuscript/SI or Figshare (doi:10.6084/m9.figshare.12356189).

_data_note: JGR paper; behind paywall. Cruise details not confirmed. Data may be at PANGAEA.

processing.summary: Sub-bottom profiler, multibeam bathymetry, and sediment cores from Aysén Fjord (~45.5°S) are analyzed to reconstruct the paleoseismic record through seismo-turbidite stratigraphy. Complex interacting seismic sources (megathrust, crustal faults, volcanic) are identified. The 2007 Aysén earthquake sequence is recognized in the sedimentary record.
workflow: 1. Sub-bottom profiler (TOPAS PS18) and multibeam (SeaBeam 1050) data acquisition during MD159-PACHIDERME cruise (2007)
2. Sediment core MD07-3117 logging with Geotek Multi-Sensor Core Logger (linescan images, MS every 2 mm using Bartington MS2E)
3. ARM/κ ratio measurements on u-channels (shielded room, LSCE France) for magnetite grain size estimation
4. CT scanning at Ghent University Hospital (Siemens SOMATOM; ~0.2 mm xy resolution, 0.3–0.6 mm z resolution)
5. CT image visualization and analysis with VGStudio 3.2 and ImageJ
6. Grain size analysis (Malvern Mastersizer 3000) after removal of organics, CaCO3, and biogenic silica
7. End-member modeling using AnalySize v1.0.2 (MATLAB-based, Weibull distributions)
8. Radiocarbon dating of 25 organic macroremains at ARTEMIS AMS facility (Saclay); 210Pb/137Cs dating of short core AY20
9. Age-depth modeling with Bacon (R package, Bayesian statistics), calibration with SHCal13
10. Tephra correlation and geochemical analysis (major elements) on CAMECA-SX Five EPMA (CAMPARIS, Paris VI)
11. Turbidite–MTD correlation with seismic stratigraphic levels from Wils et al. (2018)
12. Paleoseismic recurrence analysis and rupture mode reconstruction
13. Seismic reflection interpretation using IHS Markit Kingdom Suite (educational license)
processing.notes: JGR paleoseismology paper. Geophysical acquisition details (TOPAS PS18, SeaBeam 1050) refer to Wils et al. (2018) as primary data source. 'migration_type: TOPAS' corrected — TOPAS is the instrument name, not a processing step.

analysis_notes: PDF confirmed correct (JGR 2020, Wils et al., 24 pages including HAL cover page). Corrected: vessel R/V SONNE → R.V. Marion Dufresne (MD159-PACHIDERME); year_acquired 2014 → 2007 (core MD07-3117 retrieved before April 2007 earthquake); instruments identified (Kongsberg TOPAS PS18, ELAC SeaBeam 1050); software updated (AnalySize, VGStudio, ImageJ, Bacon, Kingdom Suite); migration_type 'TOPAS' removed (TOPAS is instrument name); Figshare data DOI added (10.6084/m9.figshare.12356189, open access); sediment core data entry added; workflow expanded to 13 steps. bounding_box confirmed 45°–46°S, 72.5°–74°W.
